author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O sing unto the Lord a new song” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The new man knoweth this, the old man knoweth it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old man is the old life, and the new man the new life: the old life is derived from Adam, the new life is formed in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in this Psalm, the whole world is enjoined to sing a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More openly elsewhere the words are these: “O sing unto the Lord a new song; sing unto the Lord, all the whole earth;” that they who cut themselves off from the communion of the whole earth, may understand that they cannot sing the new song, because it is sung in the whole, and not in a part of it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend here also, and see that this is said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when the whole earth is enjoined to sing a new song, it is meant, that peace singeth a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He hath done marvelous things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What marvelous things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, the Gospel was just now being read, and we heard the marvellous things of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The only son of his mother, who was a widow, was being carried out dead: the Lord, in compassion, made them stand still; they laid him down, and the Lord said, “Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.”…“The Lord hath done marvellous things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What marvellous things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear: “His own right hand, and His holy arm, hath healed for Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the Lord’s holy Arm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Isaiah: “Who hath believed our report, and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His holy arm then, and His own right hand, is Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ is therefore the arm of God, and the right hand of God; for this reason is it said, “hath He healed for Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not said only, “His right hand hath healed the world,” but “hath healed for Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many are healed for themselves, not for Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold how many long for that bodily health, and receive it from Him: they are healed by Him, but not for Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are they healed by Him, and not for Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they have received health, they become wanton: they who when sick were chaste, when cured become adulterers: they who when in illness injured no man, on the recovery of their strength attack and crush the innocent: they are healed, but not unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is he who is healed unto Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is healed inwardly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is he that is healed inwardly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who trusteth in Him, that when he shall have been healed inwardly, reformed into a new man, afterwards this mortal flesh too, which doth languish for a time, may in the end itself even recover its most perfect health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore be healed for Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that we may be healed for Him, let us believe in His right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath made known His salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This very right hand, this very arm, this very salvation, is our Lord Jesus Christ of whom it is said, “And all flesh shall see the salvation of God;” of whom also that Simeon who embraced the Infant in his arms, spoke, “Lord, now lettest Thou Thy servant depart in peace; for mine eyes have seen Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath made known His salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom did He make it known?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To a part, or to the whole?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to any part specially.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man betray, no man deceive, no man say, “Lo, here is Christ, or there:” the man who saith, Lo, He is here, or there, pointeth to some particular spots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom “hath the Lord declared His salvation”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth: “His righteousness hath He openly showed in the sight of the heathen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ is the right hand of God, the arm of God, the salvation of God, and the righteousness of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath remembered His mercy to Jacob, and His truth unto the house of Israel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this, “He hath remembered His mercy and truth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath pitied, so that He promised; because He promised and showed His mercy, truth hath followed: mercy hath gone before promise, promise hath been fulfilled in truth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His truth unto the house of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is this Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That ye may not perchance think of one nation of the Jews, hear what followeth: “All the ends of the world have seen the salvation of our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not said, all the earth: but, “all the ends of the world:” as it is said, from one end to the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man cut this down, let no man scatter it abroad; strong is the unity of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who gave so great a price, hath bought the whole: “All the ends of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they have seen, then, “Make a joyful noise unto the Lord, all ye lands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye already know what it is to make a joyful noise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoice, and speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye cannot express your joy, shout ye; let the shout manifest your joy, if your speech cannot: yet let not joy be mute; let not your heart be silent respecting its God, let it not be mute concerning His gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou speakest to thyself, unto thyself art thou healed; if His right hand hath healed thee for Him, speak thou unto Him for whom thou hast been healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing, rejoice, and make melody.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make melody unto the Lord upon the harp: on the harp and with the voice of a Psalm” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise Him not with the voice only; take up works, that ye may not only sing, but work also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who singeth and worketh, maketh melody with psaltery and upon the harp.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now see what sort of instruments are next spoken of, in figure: “With ductile trumpets also, and the sound of the pipe of horn” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are ductile trumpets, and pipes of horn?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ductile trumpets are of brass: they are drawn out by hammering; if by hammering, by being beaten, ye shall be ductile trumpets, drawn out unto the praise of God, if ye improve when in tribulation: tribulation is hammering, improvement is the being drawn out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Job was a ductile trumpet, when suddenly assailed by the heaviest losses, and the death of his sons, become like a ductile trumpet by the beating of so heavy tribulation, he sounded thus: “The Lord gave, and the Lord hath taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did he sound?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How pleasantly doth his voice sound?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This ductile trumpet is still under the hammer.…We have heard how he was hammered; let us hear how he soundeth: let us, if it please you, hear the sweet sound of this ductile trumpet: “What! shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O courageous, O sweet sound! whom will not that sound awake from sleep? whom will not confidence in God awake, to march to battle fearlessly against the devil; not to struggle with his own strength, but His who proveth him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He it is who hammereth: for the hammer could not do so of itself.…See how (I dare so speak, my brethren) even the Apostle was beaten with this very hammer: he saith, “there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan, to buffet me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold he is under the hammer: let us hear how he speaketh of it: “For this thing,” he saith, “I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for My strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, saith His Maker, wish to make this trumpet perfect; I cannot do so unless I hammer it; in weakness is strength made perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear now the ductile trumpet itself sounding as it should: “When I am weak, then am I strong.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The voice of the pipe of horn, what is it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The horn riseth above the flesh: in rising above the flesh it needs must be solid so as to last, and able to speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it hath surpassed the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who wisheth to be a horn trumpet, let him overcome the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this, let him overcome the flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him surpass the desires, let him conquer the lusts of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the horn trumpets.…What meaneth this, “Set your affection on things above”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It meaneth, Rise above the flesh, think not of carnal things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were not yet horn trumpets, to whom he now spoke thus: “I could not speak unto you, brethren, as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have fed you with milk, and not with meat; for hitherto ye were not able to bear it: neither yet now are ye able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye are yet carnal.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were not therefore horn trumpets, because they had not risen above the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Horn both adhereth to the flesh, and riseth above the flesh; and although it springeth from the flesh, yet it surpasseth it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore thou art spiritual, when before thou wast carnal; as yet thou art treading the earth in the flesh, but in spirit thou art rising into heaven; for though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh.…Brethren, do not reproach brethren whom the mercy of God hath not yet converted; know that as long as ye do this, ye savour of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is not a trumpet which pleaseth the ears of God: the trumpet of boastfulness maketh the war fruitless.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the horn trumpet raise thy courage against the devil; let not the fleshly trumpet raise thy pride against thy brother.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make a joyful noise in the sight of the Lord the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While ye are rejoicing, and delighted with the ductile trumpets, and the voice of the horn, what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the sea be stirred up, and the fulness thereof” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, when the Apostles, like ductile trumpets and horns, were preaching the truth, the sea was stirred up, its waves arose, tempests increased, persecutions of the Church took place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence hath the sea been stirred up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a joyful noise was made, when Psalms of thanksgiving were being sung before God: the ears of God were pleased, the waves of the sea were raised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the sea be stirred up, and the fulness thereof: the round world, and all that dwell therein.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the sea be stirred up in its persecutions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the floods clap their hands together” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the sea be aroused, and the floods clap their hands together; persecutions arise, and the saints rejoice in God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall the floods clap their hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to clap their hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To rejoice in works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To clap hands, is to rejoice; hands, mean works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What floods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those whom God hath made floods, by giving them that Water, the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If any man thirst,” saith He, “let him come unto Me, and drink.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that believeth on Me, out of his bosom shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These rivers clapped their hands, these rivers rejoiced in works, and blessed God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The hills shall be joyful together.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Before the Lord, for He is come; for He is come to judge the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The hills” signify the great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord cometh to judge the earth, and they rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are hills, who, when the Lord is coming to judge the earth, shall tremble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are therefore good and evil hills; the good hills, are spiritual greatness; the bad hills, are the swelling of pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the hills be joyful together before the Lord, for He is come; for He is come to judge the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore shall He come, and how shall He come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With righteousness shall He judge the world, and the people with equity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the hills therefore rejoice; for He shall not judge unrighteously.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When some man is coming as a judge, to whom the conscience cannot lie open, even innocent men may tremble, if from him they expect a reward for virtue, or fear the penalty of condemnation; when He shall come who cannot be deceived, let the hills rejoice, let them rejoice fearlessly; they shall be enlightened by Him, not condemned; let them rejoice, because the Lord will come to judge the world with equity; and if the righteous hills rejoice, let the unrighteous tremble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But behold, He hath not yet come: what need is there they should tremble?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them mend their ways, and rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is in thy power in what way thou willest to await the coming of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason He delayeth to come, that when He cometh He may not condemn thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, He hath not yet come: He is in heaven, thou on earth: He delayeth His coming, do not thou delay wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His coming is hard to the hard of heart, soft to the pious.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See therefore even now what thou art: if hard of heart, thou canst soften; if thou art soft, even now rejoice that He will come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou art a Christian.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, thou sayest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I believe that thou prayest, and sayest, “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou desirest Him to come, whose coming thou fearest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reform thyself, that thou mayest not pray against thyself.
